criteria.label: State Anthropic's mission in one sentence and name the corporate structure that encodes public benefit.
criteria.example: Mission: beneficial, reliable AI for society. Structure: public-benefit corporation (PBC).

criteria.label: Explain one way mission shows up in Claude product behavior (not benchmarks).
criteria.example: Policy-based refusals on disallowed requests reflect safety mission operationalized in Usage Policy.

criteria.label: Contrast PBC structure with a nonprofit and with a standard for-profit—one sentence each.
criteria.example: Nonprofit: tax-exempt charity. PBC: for-profit with charter benefit duty. Standard C-corp: profit primary without encoded public benefit.

criteria.label: Name one mission misconception and correct it with a concrete product fact.
criteria.example: Myth: mission means never refusing. Reality: harmful-use refusals are intentional—e.g., malware how-to requests may be declined.
